metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

The scenario's two Expected bullets are (1) the session appears in `ready` within ~1s and (2) the PID is bound. Both are observable for the gemini-cli adapter, with one adapter-specific nuance: Gemini does NOT write its per-session transcript at launch — it creates `~/.gemini/tmp/<project>/chats/session-<ts>-<id>.jsonl` only after the first user message lands. So the daemon's process scanner discovers the `gemini` PID at the first tick (a proc-<PID> pre-session row), and the transcript-keyed session is born + settles to `ready` once the first turn completes. Gemini has no explicit turn_done marker, so the parser settles to `ready` on a `gemini` message that carries non-empty text and opens no tool calls (heuristic). The node process tree has two pids; the heap-bump worker is excluded so the bound PID is the real CLI.
caveats: Gemini mints its own sessionId and cannot accept a pre-assigned UUID; the driver harvests it from the transcript header on line 1.
Transcript appears only after the first user message, so session_birth/ready anchor to the first turn rather than to bare agent launch; PID discovery anchors to launch.

sources.note: Identity.Name=gemini, FilesUnderRoot under ~/.gemini/tmp/<project>/chats/, 2-proc node matcher excludes the heap-bump worker.

preconditions: gemini CLI on PATH (0.46.0) and GEMINI_API_KEY exported (driver forces api-key auth via a workspace .gemini/settings.json)
irrlichd is recording — run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a daemon started with --record
recipe.prompt: Reply with exactly the word: ok

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok

verify: events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered event for the gemini process within ~1000 ms of agent launch
events.jsonl contains a transcript_new + state_transition to ready once the first turn completes
